raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 0.7,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.3
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.4", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "By 'make this stronger', do you mean to strengthen the content and arguments (e.g., conceptual framing, logic, evidence, novelty) or to improve the writing style and flow?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Conceptual reconstruction is central as reframing the thesis and contribution can make the framework's value proposition stronger.",
    "R1.2": "Logical consistency is central to ensure the argument chain is robust, thereby strengthening the overall reasoning in the text.",
    "R1.3": "Evidence-claim alignment is central to bolster claims with reported results, making the empirical support more convincing.",
    "R1.4": "Novelty assessment is central to highlight the delta over prior work, enhancing the perceived innovation of CausalRoute.",
    "R1.6": "Writing polish is central to improve sentence-level clarity and conciseness, making the text more impactful and persuasive.",
    "RX.1": "Uncertainty disclosure is central to responsibly strengthen claims by transparently addressing potential limitations.",
    "RX.2": "Overclaim avoidance is load-bearing to prevent exaggerating results while attempting to make the text stronger.",
    "RX.3": "Scope adherence is central to keep enhancements within the described framework without unwarranted expansions.",
    "RX.4": "Downstream-harm avoidance is central given the AI context, ensuring strengthening does not promote misuse.",
    "RX.5": "Provenance and traceability is peripheral but included to maintain accountability in any revisions."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: By 'make this stronger', do you mean to strengthen the content and arguments (e.g., conceptual framing, logic, evidence, novelty) or to improve the writing style and flow?

rationale.R1.1: Conceptual reconstruction is central as reframing the thesis and contribution can make the framework's value proposition stronger.
rationale.R1.2: Logical consistency is central to ensure the argument chain is robust, thereby strengthening the overall reasoning in the text.
rationale.R1.3: Evidence-claim alignment is central to bolster claims with reported results, making the empirical support more convincing.
rationale.R1.4: Novelty assessment is central to highlight the delta over prior work, enhancing the perceived innovation of CausalRoute.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is central to improve sentence-level clarity and conciseness, making the text more impactful and persuasive.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty disclosure is central to responsibly strengthen claims by transparently addressing potential limitations.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim avoidance is load-bearing to prevent exaggerating results while attempting to make the text stronger.
rationale.RX.3: Scope adherence is central to keep enhancements within the described framework without unwarranted expansions.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance is central given the AI context, ensuring strengthening does not promote misuse.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability is peripheral but included to maintain accountability in any revisions.
